clinical_coherence.notes: 41M African American musician with bicuspid aortic valve, recent dental extraction with incomplete antibiotic course, presenting with 6 weeks of constitutional symptoms (fever, weight loss, night sweats) and acute stroke. Vitals show sinus tachycardia, fever 38.4, wide pulse pressure (142/48) indicating aortic regurgitation. Peripheral stigmata of endocarditis (Osler nodes, Janeway lesions, Roth spots, splinter hemorrhages). Flawless clinical picture.

tool_output_consistency.notes: Perfectly concordant. MRI shows multi-territory bilateral infarcts (embolic shower pattern). Blood cultures positive for S. mutans (4/4 bottles). TEE shows 1.8 cm vegetation on bicuspid aortic valve with moderate AR. Labs show leukocytosis, elevated inflammatory markers, RBC casts in urine. Follow-up MRA reveals developing mycotic aneurysm. Autoimmune panel shows immune complex-mediated findings (low complement, positive RF, positive CIC). All consistent with subacute bacterial endocarditis.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD I33.0 (acute endocarditis) is appropriate for the primary cardiac diagnosis. Critical actions are excellent: blood cultures before antibiotics, echo, empiric IV antibiotics, DO NOT give tPA (high hemorrhagic risk in endocarditis), monitor for mycotic aneurysm, CT surgery consultation. Red herrings are well-documented. Differential is well-calibrated.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Outstanding puzzle. The stroke itself is secondary to infective endocarditis, requiring recognition of the systemic illness driving the embolic events. Key diagnostic clues (incomplete dental antibiotic course, bicuspid valve, constitutional symptoms, wide pulse pressure, peripheral stigmata, multi-territory infarcts) must be synthesized. Multiple red herrings (IVDU denial, positive RF suggesting autoimmune). Expected agent confidence 0.40 is appropriate.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 41M musician (gigging bass player) with bicuspid aortic valve (1-2% of population), recent dental extraction with non-compliance to antibiotics ('felt fine' after 3 days). Moderate alcohol use. Healthy BMI. No traditional stroke risk factors. Completely believable patient profile for non-IVDU native valve endocarditis.

red_herring_quality.notes: Three excellent red herrings: (1) Patient denies IVDU -- may lead to premature dismissal of endocarditis; (2) Positive rheumatoid factor on immunology panel -- may suggest primary autoimmune/vasculitis; (3) Ibuprofen use -- irrelevant but could distract. All well-documented with clear correct interpretations. The IVDU denial red herring is particularly effective given common clinical biases.

educational_value.notes: Exceptional. Teaches multi-territory embolic pattern recognition, endocarditis as stroke etiology, modified Duke criteria, tPA contraindication in endocarditis (20-40% hemorrhagic transformation), mycotic aneurysm surveillance, immune complex-mediated disease, and the critical importance of blood cultures before antibiotics. The dental procedure portal of entry and incomplete antibiotic course are clinically authentic.

strengths: Brilliant diagnostic puzzle requiring systemic disease recognition
Multi-territory infarct pattern as embolic shower hallmark
Complete endocarditis workup including Duke criteria elements
Immune complex disease features (Osler nodes, low complement, positive RF)
Realistic dental procedure as portal of entry
